python.q: Which keyword is used to define a function in Python?
python.explain: 'def' is the reserved keyword used to declare a function in Python.

python.q: What is the output of: print(2 ** 3 ** 2) ?
python.explain: The ** operator is right-associative, so it evaluates as 2 ** (3 ** 2) = 2 ** 9 = 512.

python.q: Which data type is mutable in Python?
python.explain: Lists are mutable — you can change, add, or remove items after creation.

python.q: What does the 'self' keyword represent inside a class method?
options: The current instance of the class
python.explain: 'self' refers to the current instance of the class on which the method is being called.

python.q: Which of the following is NOT a valid Python data type?
python.explain: 'array' is not a built-in primitive type — it must be imported from the 'array' module. dict, set and tuple are built-in.
The string 'Python' has 6 characters: P, y, t, h, o, n.

python.q: Which method is used to add an element at the end of a list?
python.explain: list.append(x) adds element x to the end of the list.

python.q: What is the correct way to handle exceptions in Python?
python.explain: Python uses the try-except construct for exception handling, not try-catch.

python.q: Which symbol is used for single-line comments in Python?
python.explain: The # symbol starts a single-line comment in Python.
'pass' is a null statement used as a placeholder where code is syntactically required but no action is desired.

python.q: Which built-in function converts a string to an integer?
python.explain: int('42') converts the string '42' to the integer 42.

python.q: What is the output of: print(type([])) ?
python.explain: [] denotes an empty list, so its type is <class 'list'>.

python.q: Which OOP concept allows a child class to redefine a method of its parent class?
python.explain: Method overriding lets a subclass provide a specific implementation of a method already defined in its superclass.
The 'in' operator returns True if a value is found in the given sequence (list, string, tuple, etc.).

python.q: Which keyword is used to create a generator function?
python.explain: Functions that use 'yield' instead of 'return' become generators — they produce values lazily.

data_science.q: Which Python library is mainly used for numerical computing with arrays?
data_science.explain: NumPy provides the n-dimensional ndarray and is the foundation for numerical computing in Python.

data_science.q: Which library is best for creating DataFrames?
data_science.explain: Pandas introduces the DataFrame — a 2-D labelled tabular data structure.

data_science.q: What does the term 'overfitting' mean in machine learning?
options: Model fits training data too well and fails on new data
data_science.explain: Overfitting happens when a model learns noise and details of the training data so well that it performs poorly on unseen data.

data_science.q: Which algorithm is used for classification problems?
data_science.explain: Logistic Regression is a classification algorithm despite its name — it predicts probabilities for class labels.
CSV = Comma Separated Values — a simple plain-text format for tabular data.

data_science.q: Which measure is most sensitive to outliers?
data_science.explain: Mean is heavily affected by outliers because every value contributes to the sum.

data_science.q: What is the primary goal of EDA (Exploratory Data Analysis)?
data_science.explain: EDA helps to summarise the main characteristics of data — patterns, anomalies, distributions — before any modelling.

data_science.q: Which type of plot is best to show the distribution of a single numeric variable?
data_science.explain: A histogram groups values into bins and is ideal for showing distribution of a continuous variable.

data_science.q: Which sklearn function splits data into train and test sets?
data_science.explain: sklearn.model_selection.train_test_split is the canonical helper to split a dataset.

data_science.q: Which is an UNSUPERVISED learning algorithm?
data_science.explain: K-Means groups data into clusters without labels, making it unsupervised.

data_science.q: What does 'NaN' mean in a Pandas DataFrame?
data_science.explain: NaN stands for 'Not a Number' and is used to represent missing or undefined data.

data_science.q: Which metric is used to evaluate regression models?
data_science.explain: MSE (and RMSE) measures the average squared difference between predicted and actual values — used for regression.

data_science.q: Which library is most commonly used for deep learning?
data_science.explain: TensorFlow (and PyTorch) are the leading deep-learning frameworks.

options: Bringing all features to a similar range
data_science.explain: Feature scaling (e.g. standardisation, normalisation) brings features to a comparable range to help algorithms converge faster.

data_science.q: Which file format is most efficient for big data storage?
data_science.explain: Parquet is a columnar, compressed format optimised for analytical workloads on large datasets.

java.q: Which keyword is used to inherit a class in Java?
java.explain: The 'extends' keyword is used for class inheritance; 'implements' is for interfaces.

java.q: Which is NOT a primitive data type in Java?
java.explain: String is a class (java.lang.String), not a primitive. The primitives are byte, short, int, long, float, double, char, boolean.

java.q: What is the entry point of every Java program?
java.explain: JVM calls public static void main(String[] args) to start execution.

java.q: Which OOP concept is achieved using interfaces?
java.explain: Interfaces hide implementation details and expose only behaviour — that's abstraction.
A method declared 'final' cannot be overridden by subclasses.

java.q: Which collection class allows duplicate values and maintains insertion order?
java.explain: ArrayList is an ordered list that allows duplicates.
JVM = Java Virtual Machine — it executes Java bytecode.

java.q: Which operator is used for comparing two values for equality?
java.explain: == compares primitive values (and references for objects). .equals() compares object contents.

java.q: Which exception is thrown when dividing an integer by zero?
java.explain: Integer division by zero throws java.lang.ArithmeticException.

java.q: Which keyword is used to refer to the current object?
java.explain: 'this' refers to the current instance inside an instance method or constructor.

java.q: What is the size of an int in Java?
java.explain: int is always 32 bits (4 bytes) in Java regardless of platform.

java.q: Which keyword is used to create an object in Java?
java.explain: The 'new' keyword allocates memory and invokes the constructor: e.g., new Car().
String is part of java.lang, which is imported automatically by every Java program.

java.q: Which feature of OOP shows the same method behaving differently?
java.explain: Polymorphism (overloading & overriding) allows the same method name to behave differently in different contexts.

java.q: What is the default value of a boolean variable in Java?
java.explain: Instance boolean variables default to false.

full_stack.explain: HTML = HyperText Markup Language — the standard markup language for documents on the Web.

full_stack.q: Which CSS property changes the text colour?
full_stack.explain: The 'color' property sets the foreground text colour of an element.

full_stack.q: Which is a JavaScript framework / library?
full_stack.explain: React is a JavaScript UI library. Django/Flask are Python, Laravel is PHP.

full_stack.q: Which HTTP method is used to send data to create a new resource?
full_stack.explain: POST creates a new resource on the server.
MongoDB is a document-oriented NoSQL database.
API = Application Programming Interface — a contract that lets software components communicate.
HTTP 404 indicates the requested resource was not found on the server.

full_stack.q: Which symbol is used for ID selector in CSS?
full_stack.explain: An ID selector starts with '#' followed by the id value, e.g. #header.

full_stack.q: Which JS keyword declares a block-scoped variable?
full_stack.explain: 'let' creates a block-scoped variable; 'var' is function-scoped.

full_stack.q: Which command starts a Node.js project with package.json?
full_stack.explain: 'npm init' (or 'npm init -y' for defaults) creates a package.json.

full_stack.q: Which language runs natively in the browser?
full_stack.explain: Browsers ship with a JavaScript engine — JS runs natively in every modern browser.
CSS = Cascading Style Sheets — the stylesheet language used to describe presentation of HTML.

full_stack.q: Which tag is used to link an external CSS file?
full_stack.explain: <link rel='stylesheet' href='styles.css'> is placed in <head> to link an external CSS file.

full_stack.q: Which is a popular backend framework for Node.js?
full_stack.explain: Express.js is the most widely used minimal web framework for Node.js.

full_stack.q: Which protocol is used for secure communication?
full_stack.explain: HTTPS = HTTP over TLS/SSL — encrypted secure version of HTTP.
